content.p: The master, person in charge, owner, charterer, manager, or operator of a ship involved in an incident shall report the incident in the manner prescribed by Article 8 of the Convention in accordance with regulations promulgated by the Secretary for that purpose.

subsection.heading: Requirement to report discharge, probable discharge, or presence of oil
subsection.chapeau: The master or person in charge of—

paragraph.content: a ship of United States registry or nationality, or operated under the authority of the United States, wherever located;
another ship while in the navigable waters of the United States; or
a sea port or oil handling facility subject to the jurisdiction of the United States,

subsection.continuation: shall report a discharge, probable discharge, or presence of oil in the manner prescribed by Article 4 of the International Convention on Oil Pollution Preparedness, Response and Cooperation, 1990 (adopted at London, November 30, 1990), in accordance with regulations promulgated by the Secretary for that purpose.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1991—Pub. L. 102–241 amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows:
“(a) As soon as he has knowledge of an incident, the master or other person in charge of a ship shall report it to the Secretary in the manner prescribed by Article 8 of the Convention.

“(b) Upon receipt of the report of an incident involving a ship, other than one of United States registry or nationality or one operated under the authority of the United States, the Secretary shall take the action required by Article 8 of the Convention.”
